board.title: Computer Graphics
board.author: Julius Boateng
board.description: Computer graphics began in the 1950s, expanded rapidly in hardware during the 70s, then became mainstream in the 80s through PCs, video games, and CGI films. The field relies heavily on linear algebra and geometry to render images efficiently.

clues.question: Geometric point used to define a graphics primitive
Array used for graphics transformations; fundamental to linear algebra
Rendering process converting primitives like points, lines, and polygons into pixels; developed in the 1960s
Graphics program computing color, lighting, and texture during rendering
Process enabling 3D graphics to be displayed on 2D screens
Intermediate per-pixel data generated during rasterization
Polygon primitive most efficiently rendered by GPUs; foundational to 3D graphics
Depth buffer used to determine visible surfaces by tracking distance from the camera
Rendering optimization process skipping unseen geometry
Rendering process trimming geometry to the viewing region
Mathematical object storing direction and magnitude
Smallest unit of a raster image; short for "picture element"
Vector perpendicular to a surface; essential for lighting
Computer graphics pioneer behind Sketchpad and a classic clipping algorithm; recipient of the 1988 Turing Award
Computer graphics pioneer credited with z-buffering and texture mapping; co-founded Pixar
Collection of primitives defining a 3D object
